name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
resistance_profile: Presses the front delt through a full overhead range; dumbbells allow a deep stretch.
rom_notes: Lower to ear level (deep stretch), press to a controlled overhead lockout.
cues: Lower under control to a full stretch
execution_steps: Set an upright bench and sit with the dumbbells resting on your thighs.
Kick the dumbbells up to shoulder height, palms facing forward, and set your back against the pad.
Brace your core and keep your ribs down.
Press the dumbbells up and slightly together to a controlled overhead lockout.
Lower under control to about ear level for a full stretch.
good_when: You want a deep front-delt stretch and full overhead range.
You are building shoulder size with a supported, back-friendly setup.
You want more range of motion than a barbell or machine allows.
bad_when: You are chasing maximal load (a barbell allows heavier pressing).
Getting heavy dumbbells into position is difficult without a spotter.
You need a fully fixed path for stability (use a machine).
